title: 3 takeaways from the Revolution’s 2-1 win over Minnesota United
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: New England Revolution defeated Minnesota United 2-1 at Gillette Stadium on May 16, 2026.
Goals from Carles Gil and Luca Langoni secured the victory despite Kelvin Yeboah's equalizer for Minnesota.
Goalkeeper Matt Turner made critical saves, helping New England hold their lead despite Minnesota's pressure.

title: SNL recap: Best moments from Will Ferrell hosting ‘Saturday Night Live’ season finale
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Will Ferrell hosted 'SNL' season 51 finale on May 16, featuring Paul McCartney as musical guest.
Ferrell parodied Jeffrey Epstein in a cold open alongside James Austin Johnson's Donald Trump.
Paul McCartney performed three songs, including 'Days We Left Behind' from his upcoming album.

title: Flashback: A look at previous times Tall Ships have sailed into Boston
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Sail Boston 2026 event scheduled from July 11-16 will feature 25+ countries' ships, celebrating the U.S. 250th anniversary.
Past Sail Boston events occurred in 1976, 1980, 1992, 2000, 2009, 2012, and 2017, involving millions of spectators.
The 2000 event saw protests regarding ships’ association with the slave trade, highlighting the event's complex cultural impact.

title: Vibe in Patriots building reportedly better than two weeks ago with Mike Vrabel
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Patriots' roster and culture improve as Mike Vrabel returns to rookie minicamp after offseason challenges.
Mike Vrabel's coaching energy improves team morale amid personal scandal; he missed NFL Draft's final day for counseling.
Patriots brace for challenging 2026 season with matchups against top teams and potential trade for wide receiver A.J. Brown.

title: Willson Contreras’s go-ahead HR, Payton Tolle’s 8 strong innings lift Red Sox past Braves 3-2
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Willson Contreras hit a decisive two-run home run in the eighth inning, leading the Red Sox to a 3-2 victory.
Payton Tolle pitched eight innings, allowing two runs and four hits as he set a career high in innings pitched.
Aroldis Chapman secured the save, overcoming a bases-loaded situation in the ninth to ensure the Red Sox's win.

title: Red Sox place Trevor Story on injured list with sports hernia, contemplating surgery
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox shortstop Trevor Story placed on 10-day IL with sports hernia, considering potential surgery.
Nick Sogard recalled from Triple-A Worcester to replace Trevor Story on the Red Sox roster.
Story has struggled with a .206 average and is tied for most errors at shortstop in the American League.

title: One driver dead in NH collision between pickup truck and school bus
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A 74-year-old man, David Peloski, died in a collision with a school bus in Marlborough, NH, Friday morning.
Thirteen students aboard the bus were unharmed; they were taken to local hospitals as a precaution.
The crash, involving a head-on collision on Route 12, is under investigation by New Hampshire State Police.

title: Carl Yastrzemski’s grandson, Braves’ Mike Yastrzemski, walks off Red Sox
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Mike Yastrzemski hit a walk-off single in the 10th inning, helping the Braves beat the Red Sox 3-2.
Red Sox pitcher Connelly Early allowed two home runs over five innings before being pulled in the fifth.
Boston's Marcelo Mayer tied the game with a solo homer in the seventh, but the team failed to score further.

title: Report: Reunion between Patriots, Stefon Diggs viewed as unlikely despite mutual interest
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Patriots' reunion with Stefon Diggs is unlikely due to impending A.J. Brown trade affecting salary cap space.
Stefon Diggs, interested in rejoining Patriots, completed a 1,045-yard season despite limited playtime last year.
Patriots must prioritize between adding Diggs or completing Brown trade given current salary commitments to players.

title: Boston police seek missing boy, 15
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Lafayette LaGrant-Gomes, 15, was last seen Tuesday near 965 Blue Hill Ave. in Dorchester, Boston police report.
Boston police describe Lafayette as Black, 5'8", thin build, brown eyes, and black hair, known to frequent local areas.
Public urged to contact Boston Police with information on LaGrant-Gomes, offering anonymous tips via CrimeStoppers channels.
